mappings.rationaleAI: Plant evolution from simple organisms to seed plants with innovations such as lignin and pollination mechanisms covers plant tissue differentiation (B), organ development and diversity of shoot and root systems (C.1, C.3), and evolution of plant organ systems (C.5).

Systematic survey of animal diversity organized by phyla covers biological form and function generally (A), classification of animal tissues (D.1), nervous and muscular tissue features across phyla (D.3, D.4), specialized organ systems (E.1), and interrelationships between organ systems (E.2).

Human body structure covering skeletal, respiratory, and nervous system anatomy addresses animal tissues for support (D.4), coordination (D.3), assimilation and transport (D.2), integumentary and skeletal organ systems (E.1.a), and digestive and respiratory organ systems (E.1.b).

Physiological mechanisms across organ systems including energy supply, homeostasis, and sensory perception address specialized organ systems relating to the environment (E.1.a) and serving cell metabolism (E.1.b), and functional interdependence between systems (E.2).

Cell diversity and differentiation into specialized types in multicellular organisms provides the cellular foundation for biological form and function (A), cells of plant tissues (B.3), and cells of animal tissues (D.6).

Morphogenesis and differentiation processes through which organs form from embryonic tissues address general biological form and function (A), interrelationships between developing organ systems (E.2), and development of organ systems (E.3).
Blood composition including hemoglobin, red cells, white cells, and platelets directly addresses tissues for transport (D.2) and other tissues including tissue fluids (D.5).
Endocrine system physiology covering hormone sources and mechanisms addresses tissues for coordination through endocrine tissues (D.3) and the endocrine system as a specialized organ system (E.1.a).
Dental anatomy including enamel, dentine, and tooth morphology across vertebrates addresses tissues for support (D.4) and the digestive organ system structures (E.1.b).

Eye structure and function across animal species including retinal anatomy and optic nerve connections addresses sensory tissues for coordination (D.3) and the nervous system as a specialized organ system relating to the environment (E.1.a).

Mammalian morphological diversity and ecological specializations illustrate the relationship between form and function (A), animal tissue organization (D), and specialized organ systems enabling endothermy and complex behaviour (E.1, E.2).
Endosymbiotic origins of eukaryotic organelles demonstrate a fundamental relationship between biological form and function (A) at the cellular level.

Fungal biology including tens of thousands of species with diverse morphological forms relates to biological form and function generally (A) and relatively undifferentiated tissues of nonvascular organisms (B.1).
